findings.message: Layering violation: route handler directly opens SessionLocal() and performs database operations, bypassing the repository layer. A repository function create_item() already exists (db/repository.py:11-16) that properly handles session lifecycle and commit semantics.
findings.suggestion: Refactor create_item() route to accept SessionDep dependency and call repository.create_item(session, payload.name) instead of directly manipulating the session. This maintains clean separation between the HTTP layer and data access layer.

findings.message: Session commit missing: session.refresh(item) is called without prior session.commit(). The context manager will close the session without persisting the Item, causing the created item to be lost. Compare with repository.create_item() which explicitly calls session.commit().
findings.suggestion: Call session.commit() before session.refresh(item), or better yet, delegate to the repository layer function which already handles this correctly.
